name: amd-network-operator
description: The AMD Network Operator simplifies deployment and management of AMD AI NICs (AINICs) in Kubernetes and works with the AMD GPU Operator for high-performance networking in AI/ML and HPC workloads.
overview:
# Overview
The AMD Network Operator automates NIC discovery and driver management for AMD AI NICs (e.g. AMD Pensando Pollara), with RDMA/RoCE support, Prometheus metrics, and dynamic resource allocation. It can be installed alongside the AMD GPU Operator on the same cluster. For a detailed overview of the operator's architecture and components, see the [Network Operator Overview](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/network-operator/en/main/overview.html).

## Key Features
### Automated Network Driver Builds
Builds and loads out-of-tree AMD NIC kernel modules (ionic, ionic_rdma, pds_core, tawk_ipc) on target nodes using KMM and Kaniko for in-cluster driver compilation.

### Device Plugin and Metrics
Deploys an AMD NIC device plugin and an optional metrics exporter with Prometheus integration.

### RDMA/RoCE Support
Enables RDMA over Converged Ethernet for high-performance inter-node communication in AI/ML and HPC workloads.

## Configuration
After enabling this operator, create a `NetworkConfig` CR to target specific nodes and trigger driver builds. The chart **does not** auto-create a `NetworkConfig` (unlike the GPU Operator's `DeviceConfig`). Choose a CR name other than `default` to avoid conflicts with the GPU Operator.

For physical NICs (PF), use the selector `feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-nic: "true"`. For SR-IOV virtual function (VF) environments, use `feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-vnic: "true"` instead.

### NetworkConfig CR (Private Registry)
Create a `NetworkConfig` CR that aligns with the `kmm-registry-credentials` secret created for the AMD KMM Operator. Replace the registry host, port, and project with your values:
```
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: amd.com/v1alpha1
kind: NetworkConfig
metadata:
  name: network
  namespace: <workspace-namespace>
spec:
  selector:
    feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-nic: "true"
  driver:
    enable: true
    blacklist: true
    image: "<registry-host>:<port>/<project>/amdnetwork_kmod"
    imageBuild:
      baseImageRegistry: "<registry-host>:<port>/<project>"
      baseImageRegistryTLS:
        insecure: false
        insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
    imageRegistrySecret:
      name: "kmm-registry-dockerconfig"
    imageRegistryTLS:
      insecure: false
      insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
  secondaryNetwork:
    cniPlugins:
      enable: true
EOF
```
- `blacklist` — when `true`, unloads the in-tree `ionic` kernel module before loading the DKMS version. Required on nodes that ship a built-in `ionic` driver. Without this, the in-tree driver claims the NIC first and the DKMS driver fails to load.
- `image` — the registry path where built NIC driver images are pushed/pulled. Do not include a tag; the operator manages tags automatically.
- `imageRegistrySecret.name` — must be `kmm-registry-dockerconfig`, which is auto-created by the AMD KMM Operator reconciler.
- `imageBuild.baseImageRegistry` — the private mirror hosting OS base images (e.g. `ubuntu:24.04`) used during Kaniko builds. This avoids Docker Hub rate limits.
- Set `insecure`/`insecureSkipTLSVerify` to `true` only for registries using self-signed certificates or plain HTTP.
- `secondaryNetwork.cniPlugins.enable` — must be `true` to deploy the `amd-host-device` CNI plugin binary to `/opt/cni/bin` on each node. Without this, Multus cannot attach NICs to pods (`failed to find plugin "amd-host-device"`).

### Config Overrides (Helm)
The Helm chart exposes controller-level settings (image, resources, tolerations, etc.) via config overrides. The `NetworkConfig` CR above is **not** managed by Helm and must be created separately. For the full list of Helm overrides, see [values.yaml](https://github.com/ROCm/network-operator/blob/v1.2.0/helm-charts-k8s/values.yaml).

## Deploying Workloads with Network Devices
Once the operator is installed and NIC drivers are loaded, workloads can request AMD NIC/vNIC devices. This requires Multus CNI and a `NetworkAttachmentDefinition`.

### 1. Create a NetworkAttachmentDefinition
```
apiVersion: k8s.cni.cncf.io/v1
kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition
metadata:
  name: amd-host-device-nad
  annotations:
    k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/resourceName: amd.com/vnic
spec:
  config: '{
    "name": "amd-host-device-nad",
    "cniVersion": "0.3.1",
    "type": "amd-host-device"
  }'
```

### 2. Request NIC Resources in the Workload
Annotate pods with `k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: amd-host-device-nad` and request `amd.com/nic` or `amd.com/vnic` in resource limits:
```
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: amd-host-device-nad
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        resources:
          requests:
            amd.com/nic: 1
          limits:
            amd.com/nic: 1
        securityContext:
          capabilities:
            add: [IPC_LOCK, NET_ADMIN, NET_RAW]
```
For full details including IB and RCCL test examples, see [Deploy a Workload with a Network Device](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/network-operator/en/main/installation/workload.html).

## Troubleshooting: In-Tree Module Conflict
The `driver.blacklist: true` setting in the NetworkConfig prevents the in-tree `ionic` kernel module from loading on reboot. However, if the module was already loaded before the operator was installed, the blacklist has no effect until the next reboot. A node reboot is required for the blacklist to take effect in this case.

## Dependencies
- **AMD KMM Operator** — provides the shared KMM instance. Strongly recommended to enable first; without it, driver builds will not function. To use the chart's embedded KMM subchart instead of the shared catalog app, set `kmm.enabled: true` in your config overrides and skip enabling `amd-kmm-operator`. You must also manually create the `kmm-registry-dockerconfig` secret (normally auto-created by the KMM Operator reconciler) with your registry credentials — see [Registry Secret Configuration](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/drivers/installation.html#registry-secret-configuration).
- **Node Feature Discovery** — provided by the NKP platform layer (disabled in this chart).
- **Multus CNI** — required for `NetworkAttachmentDefinition` support. Provided by NKP v2.18+ (disabled in this chart). For NKP v2.17.x and earlier, add `multus.enabled: true` to the Helm config overrides.

## More Information

- [AMD Network Operator - GitHub](https://github.com/ROCm/network-operator)
